recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the southeastern United States within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: S1 shows only a tropical depression TWO-26 active offshore in the Atlantic with no signal indicating a cyclone landfall in the southeastern US, and S2 is in the East Pacific.

recent.statement: The Federal Reserve will raise interest rates at the next FOMC meeting on July 28th
recent.evidence: The FOMC meeting is confirmed scheduled for 2026-07-28, which is after the window closed, and no signal shows any rate increase occurred.

recent.statement: Tropical Depression TWO-26 will make landfall in the southeastern United States.
recent.evidence: S1 shows TWO-26 as an active Atlantic tropical depression but never states it made landfall in the southeastern US, and no other signal captures such an event within the window.

recent.statement: The VIX fear gauge will rise above 20
recent.evidence: The VIX never rose above 20 during the window, peaking at 18.51 and closing at 17.83, well below the threshold.

recent.statement: The FOMC meeting on July 28 will result in a rate hike.
recent.evidence: The FOMC meeting is scheduled for July 28, after the window closed, and no signal shows any rate hike occurred.

recent.statement: The US Federal Reserve will raise interest rates at the upcoming FOMC meeting on July 28th.
recent.evidence: S7 confirms the FOMC meeting is scheduled for 2026-07-28, eight days after the window closed, so no signal shows a rate hike actually occurred.

recent.statement: The VIX fear gauge will exceed 20 within the next 24 hours, indicating increased market volatility.
recent.evidence: All VIX readings stayed below 20 (peaking at 18.51), and the latest snapshot shows 18.22, so the threshold was never exceeded.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the southeastern United States within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: Neither signal shows a tropical cyclone making landfall in the southeastern US; S2 (Fausto) was in the East Pacific and S1 was an Atlantic depression with no stated landfall.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.8 earthquake will occur in the southern part of Mexico within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: S1 reports a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in Mexico, below the forecast's 5.8 threshold, so the predicted event did not occur.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6+ earthquake will strike the west coast of Mexico within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: S1 reports only a 5.5M earthquake in Mexico, below the required magnitude 6+ threshold, and no signal shows a 6+ event.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the United States within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: S2 shows FAUSTO-26 active in the East Pacific but nothing indicates a landfall in the United States within the window.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in the United States within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The signals only show a tropical depression (TWO-26) and an East Pacific tropical storm (FAUSTO-26) as active green (low-impact) notifications, with no signal indicating a US landfall.
